title: Jasione ateridae
intro: Jasione ateridae, also known as Blue Jasione or Alpine Bluebell, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Jasione ateridae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and as a general tonic to boost the immune system.

Jasione ateridae is likely not a valid scientific name for any plant species. However, the Jasione genus is classified within the family Campanulaceae. Members of the Jasione genus are commonly known as mountain bluet or chalk sea-blue flowers. They are native to the Mediterranean region, Europe, and western Asia.
